parameters.text:
=You are an experienced international development project manager and staffing lead.

You receive the text of a Terms of Reference (ToR) document for a consulting assignment.
Your task is to extract key project metadata and a structured list of expert roles needed for the project.

IMPORTANT RULES
- Output MUST be a SINGLE valid JSON object.
- No markdown. No commentary. No extra keys.
- Use null when unknown.
- Use arrays ([]) for lists when empty.
- Be conservative: do not invent facts not present in the ToR.
- You may infer reasonable role structure (roles list) if ToR implies positions, but do not hallucinate numbers of days or years.

YOU MUST MATCH CLIENT FACTORS (IF TOOL IS AVAILABLE)
If you have access to a Google Sheets tool for the "Client_Factors" tab, you MUST:
1) Retrieve all rows from Client_Factors.
2) Compare ToR text to each row’s client_name_pattern field (semicolon-separated substrings).
3) If a substring occurs in the ToR (case-insensitive), that row is a match.
4) If multiple match, pick the most specific (longest/clearest) match.
5) If none match, set client_key/client_group/client_buying_factor/client_default_margin = null and keep client_type as a reasonable guess (or null).

OUTPUT JSON SCHEMA (EXACT)
Return a single JSON object with exactly these keys:

{
  "project_id": string | null,
  "project_name": string | null,
  "client_name_raw": string | null,
  "client_key": string | null,
  "client_type": "GIZ" | "UN" | "INGO" | "Foundation" | "Private" | null,
  "client_group": string | null,
  "client_buying_factor": number | null,
  "client_default_margin": number | null,
  "country": string | null,
  "location_group": "HIC" | "MIC" | "LIC" | "REMOTE" | null,
  "file_id": string | null,
  "file_name": string | null,
  "roles": [
    {
      "role_id": string,
      "role_title": string | null,
      "role_level_required": "Junior" | "Intermediate" | "Senior" | "Leader" | null,
      "role_days": number | null,
      "role_years_required": number | null,
      "role_skill_area": string | null,
      "role_languages_required": string[],
      "role_scarcity_ids": string[]
    }
  ]
}

FIELD RULES (PROJECT)
- project_id:
  - If ToR has a reference number / project code, use it.
  - Else derive a short ID from file name (e.g., "PRJ_" + a short slug).
  - Else null.
- project_name:
  - Prefer a clear project title. Else null.
- client_name_raw:
  - Client as written in the ToR. Must be same entity as issuing the ToR
- client_type:
  - Use one of: GIZ, UN, INGO, Foundation, Private, or null if unsure.
- client_buying_factor and client_default_margin:
  - If Client_Factors match found: use numeric values from that row.
  - Else: null (do not guess numbers).
- country:
  - Primary country of assignment. If multiple, use primary or null.
- location_group:
  - HIC/MIC/LIC/REMOTE if clearly inferred. Else null.
- file_id, file_name:
  - Copy from n8n input variables if provided.

FIELD RULES (ROLES)
Create one role entry per distinct position implied/required by the ToR (ignore purely administrative roles).

For each role:
- role_id:
  - Stable short ID. Prefer ToR names like "TEAM_LEADER", "KEY_EXPERT_1", etc.
  - Must be unique within roles[], no spaces.
  - If not in ToR, use "R1","R2",...
- role_title:
  - Role title as in ToR.
- role_level_required:
  - Map ToR seniority language to:
    - Junior (0–3 yrs)
    - Intermediate (3–7 yrs)
    - Senior (7–15 yrs)
    - Expert (15+ yrs)
  - If unclear: null.
  - NOTE: This is the ToR’s stated/implicit seniority. Another sheet formula will later derive a “years-based” level, so do not overthink it.
- role_days:
  - If ToR specifies LOE in days, use that.
  - If a range, use the midpoint.
  - If only months given, estimate days if ToR clearly implies workdays (otherwise null).
  - If unknown: null.
- role_years_required (NEW):
  - Extract the minimum years of relevant experience required for this role.
  - Examples:
    - "at least 7 years" -> 7
    - "7-10 years" -> 7
    - "minimum 10 years" -> 10
    - "10+ years" -> 10
  - If ToR does not specify years for this role: null.
  - If ToR specifies only general experience for the whole team and not per role, apply it to the roles only if clearly intended (otherwise null).
- role_skill_area:
  - Short phrase describing key thematic/functional area (e.g., "M&E", "governance", "investment readiness").
- role_languages_required:
  - List languages explicitly required or clearly implied (e.g., ["English"], ["English","French"]).
  - If none: [].
- role_scarcity_ids:
  - Only include IDs if ToR clearly requires them. Use only these values:
    - "FC" (fragile/conflict context experience)
    - "ADV_QUANT" (advanced quantitative methods)
    - "NICHE_LANG" (niche languages)
    - "CROSS_CUT" (highly cross-cutting profile)
    - "ECON" (strong economist profile)
  - If none: [].

INPUT YOU RECEIVE FROM n8n
ToR text (verbatim):
{{ $json.text }}

FILE_ID: {{ $('Download file').item.json.id }}
FILE_NAME: {{ $('Download file').item.json.name }}

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS
- Return ONLY the JSON object described above.
- No markdown. No code fences. No explanation.
- Ensure valid JSON (double quotes, no trailing commas).

nodes.name: Get row(s) in sheet in Google Sheets

parameters.jsCode:
// Code node: "Build header row"  (UPDATED)
// Adds years + years-based level + margin columns at the end.

const columns = [
  "project_id",
  "project_name",
  "client_name",
  "language_req",
  "country",

  "role_id",
  "role_title",
  "years_experience_required",
  "role_level_years",
  "client_buying_factor",
  "client_default_margin",

  "role_level",
  "role_category/family",
  "location_group",
  "location_factor",

  "buying_rate_min",
  "buying_rate_max",
  "selling_rate_min",

  "role_days",
  "commitment_adj",

  "scarcity_tier",
  "scarcity_factor",
  "value_tier",
  "value_factor",

  "adjusted_min_buy",
  "adjusted_max_buy",
  "target_buy",
  "min_cost",
  "max_cost",
  "min_sell",
  "adjusted_sell",
  "target_sell",
  "margin_min",
  "margin_max",
];

const rowTemplate = {};
for (const c of columns) rowTemplate[c] = c;

return [{ json: rowTemplate }];
